steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Boundaries (punctuation) question. The blank fills the word that ends one independent clause ("Philosopher Peter Kivy was a leading figure in musical ____") and the text that follows begins another independent clause ("Kivy's approach to the study of music was decidedly formalist"). Two independent clauses need a correct boundary between them.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘aesthetics as’: Using no punctuation between the two independent clauses creates a run-on sentence — two main clauses fused without any separator. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘aesthetics and as’: A coordinating conjunction joining two independent clauses requires a comma before it (", and"); without that comma, this construction is ungrammatical. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘aesthetics, as’: A comma alone between two independent clauses creates a comma splice — a comma cannot mark a sentence boundary without a coordinating conjunction. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘aesthetics. As’: A period correctly ends the first independent clause and starts a new sentence, where "As evidenced by...itself" is a properly placed adverbial modifier preceding the main clause about Kivy's formalism. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
